content:
When I started my software engineering career, I believed senior developers wrote flawless code. I imagined they never introduced bugs, never struggled with architecture, and somehow produced awesome solutions on the first attempt.
After years of working with experienced engineers, I discovered something really big and surprising.
Senior developers rarely write perfect code.
In fact, I found that many intentionally avoid chasing perfection. They are really going with the flow only!
At first, that sounds contradictory, right?
Shouldn’t more experience lead to better code? — It does, but experience also teaches something beginners often overlook: software is built for people, not just computers.
# Perfect Code Doesn’t Exist
Press enter or click to view image in full size
